Make /llms.txt Return text/markdown on GitHub Pages: A Cloudflare Transform Rule Guide

I dropped /llms.txt onto my gh-pages site, ran curl -I, and found it coming back as text/plain. This post walks through fixing that with a single Cloudflare Response Header Transform Rule — no Worker needed, free plan covers it. Covers why .txt defaults to text/plain via mime-db, the free plan's 10-rule allowance, the full Dashboard setup, how to write the match expression, and verifying with curl -I.
Discord .well-known Domain Verification Doesn't Work on GitHub Pages — Here's the Fix

I tried to set up Discord's HTTPS domain verification on my GitHub Pages-hosted Hexo blog and hit a wall — GitHub Pages flat-out ignores .well-known directories. This post walks through what went wrong, why Hexo also needed a tweak to even generate the .well-known folder, and the workaround that actually got Discord to verify: spinning up a dedicated .well-known repo as a separate GitHub Pages site.
GitHub Pages Lets You Set Up Your Own 404 Page Too

GitHub Pages actually supports a custom 404 page out of the box — no extra config needed. This post explains what a 404 page is for (UX and SEO), what GitHub Pages' default 404 looks like, and how to drop a 404.html (or 404.md with permalink) into your repo root to ship your own.
The Simplest Way to Deploy Your Own gh-pages Site

gh-pages is a free GitHub service that lets you deploy a static site for showcase purposes. For frontend devs that's super handy. But there are a bunch of ways to deploy, so this post walks through the simplest one.
Setting the Correct Build Path for Vue CLI and Vite

This post covers how to properly set the build path for Vue CLI and Vite. It actually matters a lot — if you don't set it right, both your local dev and post-build paths will break. So here's how to do it correctly.
Buy a Domain (Google Domains) and Point It at Your Blog on GitHub Pages With Cloudflare

Heads up — Google Domains was discontinued in 2023 and transferred to Squarespace. The Google Domains-specific parts of this post are historical, but the GitHub Pages + Cloudflare setup is still applicable. If you're setting up a new domain today, see my newer post on Cloudflare Domain instead. Keeping this post as historical reference for anyone who already has a Google Domain.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - Vite: Why It's So Fast & Deploying to GitHub Pages - (23)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

In the last chapter we got our Vite + React + Tailwind CSS environment ready, so now it's time to move everything over from CRA to Vite. After that, we'll take a quick look at why Vite is so fast under the hood, and walk through two different ways to deploy the project to GitHub Pages.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - Moving the TodoList into CRA and Deploying to GitHub Pages - (21)

In this chapter we're taking the TodoList we built back on day eleven and moving it over from CodePen into a real Create React App (CRA) project. Along the way we'll tidy up the CRA boilerplate, add Tailwind CSS so the TodoList actually looks decent, and then walk through deploying the whole thing to GitHub Pages step by step. By the end you'll have a real, shareable URL for your TodoList.
Vite Deployed to GitHub Pages but _plugin-vue_export-helper Returns 404

Vite builds and ships to GitHub Pages fine — but once you load the page, the dynamically imported _plugin-vue_export-helper module 404s and the page goes blank. This post walks through the actual cause (Rollup's filename sanitization producing a name GitHub Pages doesn't serve) and the vite.config.js tweak that fixes it.
GitHub Pages "Build Failure" — the .nojekyll Fix for Hexo and Static Sites

Deployed Hexo (or any static site) to GitHub Pages and got "The page build failed for the master/main branch" email? Two common causes — either GitHub Pages is having an outage, or GitHub Pages is trying to compile your site as Jekyll. This post covers the .nojekyll workaround and how to make Hexo include hidden files.
